Tile prices in Nairobi

Porcelain runs from KES 1,200 to KES 3,500 per square metre, fitted. Ceramic is cheaper, from KES 700, and it is what most Nairobi homeowners actually choose for a bathroom. The price swings on three things: the size of the tile, whether it is rectified, and how much of the old floor has to come up first. We have fitted about four hundred of these across Westlands and Kilimani, and the number that surprises people is the screed, which is routinely a third of the total and never quoted up front. Budget for it and the rest of the job stops feeling like a moving target.
